entries.title: Ephraim the Syrian on Genesis 17
entries.excerpt: “And when Abram was ninety years old and nine, the Lord appeared to Abram, and said unto him: …be thou blameless… in the covenant which I shall set with thee” (Gen 17:1–2). “And I will multiply thee exceedingly, and will make of thee nations” — that is, into many tribes; here too of course are meant the descendants of Esau, of Keturah, and of Ishmael, who became numerous peoples. “…and kings shall come out of thee,” by which were indicated kings from the tribe of Judah and Ephraim and the Idumean kings (Gen 17:6).…
